items.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

items.body:
## рџЋЇ Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue requests documentation updates to `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` README to cover four new features shipped in the 1.0.0-beta.2 release: chat isolation key enforcement, `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`, metadata validation constraints, and corrected error response shapes.

<details>
<summary>рџ“‹ Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` (1.0.0-beta.2)
**Affected API:** README.md вЂ” Key concepts, Troubleshooting/Logging, Troubleshooting/Common errors sections
**Scenarios:**
- Missing documentation for `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header multi-tenant isolation behavior
- Missing documentation for `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` and how to enable Foundry storage call logging
- Incorrect documentation of error codes (cancel-after-delete returns 404, not 400)
- No documentation for metadata map validation constraints (в‰¤16 keys, key в‰¤64 chars, value в‰¤512 chars)

**Root ask:** Update the README to accurately describe beta.2 behavioral changes so users can correctly use chat isolation, enable logging, and understand error responses.
</details>

<details>
<summary>рџ”Ћ Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only gap with no functional bug. The implementation (PR #58252, commit cc01a6c) is already shipped. Users relying on the old README may:
- Not pass the isolation key on subsequent calls, causing unexpected 404s
- Miss that `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` exists, leading to difficulty diagnosing outbound Foundry storage failures
- Incorrectly expect `400 Bad Request` from cancel-after-delete (which now returns `404`)

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` and confirm the four gaps described in the issue
2. Apply the Implementation Guide changes in the issue body (sections 1a, 1b, 1c)
3. Run `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` and `dotnet test ... --filter TestCategory!=Live` to confirm no regressions
4. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` if new snippet regions are added
</details>

<details>
<summary>рџЏ·пёЏ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` вЂ” `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` is a data-plane client SDK (not management/provisioning); the issue is about SDK documentation
- **Service:** `AI Agents` вЂ” the service directory is `sdk/agentserver/`, which maps to `%AI Agents` per CODEOWNERS line 82вЂ“86; confirmed by similar issues (e.g., #57183, #55585) using the same service label (color `e99695`)
- **Confidence:** High вЂ” the issue explicitly names the package and directory, with no ambiguity between services or categories
</details>

<details>
<summary>рџ‘Ґ Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %AI Agents` (line 85) вЂ” the predicted service label "AI Agents" matches this entry directly
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** `@ankitbko`, `@RaviPidaparthi` (line 86)
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; issue left unassigned (bot-filed, no "customer-reported" label, so "needs-team-attention" was not added); `@mentioned` ServiceOwners above
- **Scan notes:** Bottom-to-top scan starting from line 1228 вЂ” all management-specific `%Mgmt` entries (lines 912+) were skipped since the issue has no "Mgmt" label; `%AI Agents` at line 85 was the first and only match
</details>
